step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("endodontically treated posterior teeth susceptibility to fracture reasons")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered evidence, there appears to be strong support for why endodontically treated posterior teeth might indeed become more prone to fracturing compared to their untreated counterparts. Several pieces of literature highlight the issue of reduced internal support once the pulp is removed via root canal therapy, thereby diminishing inherent strength reserves found naturally even among structurally compromised teeth prior to undergoing such invasive procedures. Additionally, certain techniques employed during endodontic interventions could introduce further vulnerabilities depending on how conservatively or aggressively they're carried out - e.g., ultra-conservative approaches versus more radical modifications aimed at clearing infected areas extensively. Furthermore, residual fragments of broken-down dentin and possibly incomplete sealing materials used internally sometimes fail to offer adequate reinforcement against mechanical stresses encountered daily during mastication cycles. Lastly, although not explicitly mentioned earlier, it seems plausible given general principles governing biological materials that any alteration made to native tooth morphology would inherently compromise existing load-bearing capacities somewhat irrespective of exact nature/extent of modification implemented. Therefore, considering all points discussed thus far alongside newly acquired insights presented herein, my stance firmly aligns with Option D stating "All of the Above" represents accurate reasoning explaining higher propensity towards developing cracks/fissures in restored back teeth vis-a-vis comparable specimens preserved entirely intact biochemically undisturbed save minor superficial wear-and-tear typical aging process entails normally. Thus concluding argument favorably supports broadest interpretation encapsulating myriad interconnected factors converging cumulatively towards same ultimate endpoint manifesting observable differences statistically significant enough warrant careful consideration whenever contemplating elective endodontic therapies involving permanent alterations to complex multi-layered organic constructs constituting our precious oral cavities housing numerous irreplaceable jewels adorning smiles worldwide!
